bird.name: 秃鹫
bird.summary: 禿鷲（學名：Aegypius monachus，英語：cinereous vulture）是一種大型的猛禽，屬於鷹科，分布於大部分溫帶的歐亞大陸，體長為1.2米（3英尺11英寸），翼展達到3.1米（10英尺），最大體重為14公斤（31磅），是最大的舊大陸鷲類，也是鷹科家族中最大…

relations.subject: 秃鹫
relations.evidence: They forage for carcasses over various kinds of terrain, including steppe, other grasslands, open woodlands, along riparian habitats or any kind or gradient of mountainous habitat
relations.object_summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

relations.subject: 秃鹫
relations.evidence: Some cinereous vultures in these areas live in mountainous forests and shrubland from , while the others preferred arid or semi-arid alpine meadows and grasslands at in elevation. This species can fly at a very high altitude
relations.object_summary: 低矮木本植物占优势的陆地生境。

relations.subject: 秃鹫
relations.evidence: Some cinereous vultures in these areas live in mountainous forests and shrubland from , while the others preferred arid or semi-arid alpine meadows and grasslands at in elevation. This species can fly at a very high altitude
relations.object_summary: 东非常见的开放草地与湿地边缘复合景观。

relations.subject: 秃鹫
relations.evidence: Trapping and hunting of cinereous vultures is particularly prevalent in China and Russia, although the poaching for trophy hunting are also known for Armenia, and probably other countries in Caucasus. Perhaps an even greater threat to this desolation-loving species is development and habitat destruction
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.subject: 秃鹫
relations.evidence: Trapping and hunting of cinereous vultures is particularly prevalent in China and Russia, although the poaching for trophy hunting are also known for Armenia, and probably other countries in Caucasus. Perhaps an even greater threat to this desolation-loving species is development and habitat destruction
relations.object_summary: 直接捕杀或非法贸易导致种群下降。

relations.subject: 秃鹫
relations.evidence: Trapping and hunting of cinereous vultures is particularly prevalent in China and Russia, although the poaching for trophy hunting are also known for Armenia, and probably other countries in Caucasus. Perhaps an even greater threat to this desolation-loving species is development and habitat destruction
relations.object_summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。
